text: The video begins with the repair of a colorectal leak using transanal laparoscopy. The transanal reusable D-port is introduced into the anus, followed by the application of a silicon cap with three orifices. A 10mm, 30-degree rigid scope is used for visualization. A monocurved grasping forceps is inserted through the nine o'clock orifice, and a monocurved needle holder with suture is introduced through the three o'clock orifice. The suture is placed on the staple line at the defect site, and the repair begins with multiple suture bites. The surgeon ties an intraluminal knot to complete the first repair, using monocurve scissors to cut the suture. A second suture is started, demonstrating collision-free interluminal work and ergonomic knot tying, completing the procedure.

The surgeon performs a transanal laparoscopic repair of a late colorectal fistula. The reusable D-port is introduced into the anus, exposing the fistula. A monocurve grasping forceps and needle holder are used through the port orifices at the nine and three o'clock positions. The first suture is placed on the inferior edge of the defect, passed inside, and then through the superior edge, forming a figure of eight suture. The needle is cut using monocurve scissors, and an interluminal knot is tied. Another figure of eight suture is performed similarly, followed by a final intracorporeal knot.
The final view of the repair shows a clear lumen without stenosis, indicating successful resolution of the colorectal defect using the transanal approach.
